document.docAuthor: Olmi, Massimo, Guglielmino, Adalgisa, Vasilenko, Dmitry V. & Perkovsky, Evgeny E.
document.docTitle: Rovnodryinus khomychi Olmi & Guglielmino & Vasilenko & Perkovsky 2022, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: Discovery of the first apterous pincer wasp from amber, with description of a new tribe, genus and species of Apodryininae (Hymenoptera, Dryinidae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Discovery of the first apterous pincer wasp from amber, with description of a new tribe, genus and species of Apodryininae (Hymenoptera, Dryinidae)

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Tropical Entomology Research Center, Via De Gasperi 10. I- 01100 Viterbo, Italy

Department of Agriculture and Forestry Sciences (DAFNE), University of Tuscia, Via San Camillo de Lellis, I- 01100 Viterbo, Italy guglielm @ unitus. it; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0002 - 2541 - 5240

Cherepovets State University, Lunacharsky prospect 5, Cherepovets 162600, Vologda region, Russia & A. A. Borissiak Paleontological Institute of the Russian Academy of Sciences, Profsoyuznaya Str. 123, Moscow, 117647, Russia
I. I. Schmalhausen Institute of Zoology, National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine, B. Khmelnitskogo 15, Kiev, 01030, Ukraine perkovsk @ gmail. com; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0002 - 7959 - 4379

subSubSection.paragraph:
Material examined :
Female holotype number L-905 (deposited in SIZK ). Specimen in Upper Eocene Rovno amber, collected in Varash district , Rovno region . Horizon : Priabonian (35–38 Ma)
.
Etymology : the new species is named after the owner of the amber piece, Mr. Nikolai R. Khomych.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 2 .
Rovnodryinus khomychi
, ♀ holotype. A, antennae; B, flagellomeres 1 and 2; C, mesosoma in lateral view; D, flagellomere 4. a = ADO. Scale bar: 0.9 mm for A, 0.01 mm for B, 0.9 mm for C, 0.06 mm for D.

FIGURE 3 .
Rovnodryinus khomychi
, ♀ holotype: A, protarsus; B, chela.
Apogonatopus edaphicus
, ♀ paratype from Madagascar, Ambilanivy forest: C, dorsal view; D, lateral view. Scale bar: 0.6 mm for A, 0.2 mm for B, 1.4 mm for C and D.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Remarks . Because of the peculiar morphologic characters indicated in the above diagnosis, the tribe Rovnodryinini trib. nov. can be included in the subfamily Apodryininae . The keys to the subfamilies and tribes of Dryinidae females presented by Olmi 1984 , 1987 , Olmi & Guglielmino 2010 and
Olmi et al . 2020
can be modified by replacing the first six couplets as follows:

paragraph.keyLead:
3 Head hypognathous (Fig. 7 in Olmi & Guglielmino 2010 ); face and part of clypeus without strong median raised carina (
Fig. 5
in Olmi & Guglielmino 2010 ), with or without normal frontal line............................ 1.
Aphelopinae Perkins

- Head prognathous (
Fig. 2
in Olmi & Guglielmino 2010 ); face and part of clypeus with one strong median raised carina (
Fig. 1
in Olmi & Guglielmino 2010 )...............................................
2. Erwiniinae
Olmi & Guglielmino
4 Ocelli absent ( Figs 3C , 5A ; Fig. 15 in Olmi 1984 ); ADOs present in antennomeres 3–10 or 4–10...................... 5

5 Mesosoma almost completely composed of fused segments ( Figs 3C, 3D , 5A, 5B ); prothorax fused with mesothorax; in mesosoma, only propleuron mobile and articulated with other segments of mesosoma...................................................................................... 11.
Apodryininae Olmi
, Tribe
Apodryinini trib. nov.
(extant)

- Mesosoma partly composed of fused segments ( Figs 3 ,
4 in
Olmi 1987 ); entire prothorax mobile, articulated with rest of mesosoma........................................................................... 12. Plesiodryininae Olmi

6 Mesosoma almost completely composed of fused segments ( Figs 1A , 2C ); prothorax fused with mesothorax; in mesosoma, only propleuron mobile and articulated with other segments of mesosoma; head with ocelli situated in the anterior half of the face ( Figs 1B, C, D ); ADOs present in antennomeres 3–10..................................................................................................... 11.
Apodryininae Olmi
, Tribe
Rovnodryinini trib. nov.
(extinct)

subSubSection.paragraph: In the above key, the new tribe Rovnodryinini is distinct from the nominal tribe Apodryinini because of the presence of the ocelli (absent in Apodryinini ). Following the description of Rovnodryinini and the rules of the International Code of Zoological Nomenclature, the new tribe Apodryinini can be described as follows:
